name: Probiotics
effects: May reduce antibiotic-associated diarrhea in some strains
May modestly improve some IBS symptoms (strain dependent)
contraindications: Use caution in severely immunocompromised/ICU patients
Products are not interchangeable across strains

sources.title: Probiotics Fact Sheet for Health Professionals (NIH ODS)

description: Lactobacillus, Bifidobacterium, Saccharomyces boulardii and others show indication- and strain-specific outcomes.
summary: Live microbial products with strain-specific effects; avoid broad universal claims.
mechanism: Proposed mechanisms include microbiome modulation, barrier effects, and immune signaling changes.
safetyNotes: Rare invasive infection risk exists in high-risk patients.
interactions: Theoretical concerns with immunosuppressants and antifungals.
dosage: No universal dose; many products provide ~1–10 billion CFU/day.
preparation: Capsules/powders/fermented foods; viability and storage matter.
regulatoryStatus: Typically sold as foods or supplements, not approved drugs.
mechanisms: Proposed mechanisms include microbiome modulation, barrier effects, and immune signaling changes
